coordinate_system.primary_axis: +X along the 234.88 mm overall length in the bottom side/profile view; +Z upward from the bottom datum A mounting face; +Y across the part width / along the major socket-bore axes as represented by the plan and section views.
coordinate_system.orientation_notes: The bottom main view is treated as an X-Z side/profile view. The middle long view is treated as a plan/top view showing X-Y layout. Section A-A cuts through the long socket/body region. Section B-B cuts through bore R. Access directions are assigned from this reconstructed coordinate system, not from view names.
coordinate_system.datum_origin: Datum A is the bottom mounting surface. No explicit X/Y coordinate origin is shown.

features.name: Overall one-piece cast component body
features.description: Irregular cast component body with long base, sloped/drafted upper surfaces, integral circular boss/socket regions, central web/rib structure, and controlled mounting face. Overall dimensions read as 234.88 mm long, approximately 149.94 mm across the sectioned envelope, and 55.00 mm high.

geometry.position_description: Overall casting envelope; bottom datum A at lower edge; long dimension left-to-right in main view.

features.material_condition: Cast body; drawing notes require freedom from detrimental casting defects, trimmed/ground flash, minimum wall thickness 2.5 mm, and general edge break.

feature_graph.graph_summary: The drawing defines an irregular one-piece cast component titled COMPONENT, drawing number CM99B361510, in metric units. The reconstructed part has an overall casting envelope approximately 234.88 mm long, 149.94 mm across the sectioned width/envelope, and 55.00 mm high, with datum A as the bottom flat mounting surface controlled by flatness 0.25. The body includes two Ø35.0 external circular boss collars connected by a long central web, large machined socket bores Ø31.10/30.90, a separate Bore R Ø31.10/30.90 with position control to datums A/B/C, two Ø9.00 locating holes interpreted as datum B and datum C features, socket-clearance geometry Ø28.94 I/P, local pads/steps, drafted faces, R25 and R10 blend radii, four 1.5 +0.10/-0.25 x45° chamfers at Bore R, and a general 0.5 max edge-break requirement. General casting notes specify defect-free casting, flash trimming/grinding, 1° draft except on the mounting surface, R1.0 unless otherwise specified, minimum casting wall thickness 2.5 mm, and a general profile tolerance 1.5 to A/B/C. Several GD&T/datum details and the exact role of the Ø28.94 and Ø10.25/9.85 features are partially legible and flagged for clarification.

clarifications.question: Confirm the title-block material specification and revision/issue field. The material reads like 'MS - 12.20, OPT: MS - 12.10', and the revision field is not clearly legible.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Material and revision control purchasing, compliance, and downstream process assumptions.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use material raw text 'MS - 12.20, OPT: MS - 12.10' and leave revision unspecified until confirmed.

clarifications.question: Confirm the datum feature definitions for B and C and the exact feature-control frames on the two Ø9.00 holes and Bore R.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Datum B and C appear to be the two Ø9 locating hole axes, but the C datum symbol and some GD&T symbols are only partially legible. Incorrect datum mapping would affect inspection and tolerance interpretation.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat datum A as the bottom mounting face, datum B as the right Ø9.00 hole axis, and datum C as the left Ø9.00 hole axis.

clarifications.question: Confirm whether the drawing contains two or three final Ø31.10/30.90 socket bores and whether the 4X chamfer note applies only to Bore R or to additional bores as well.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Section A-A shows a 2X Ø31.10/30.90 callout, Section B-B shows Bore R with the same limits, and the isometric suggests multiple socket openings. Chamfer scope affects feature count and edge geometry.
clarifications.suggested_default: Model two Ø31.10/30.90 bores in Section A-A plus one Bore R Ø31.10/30.90; apply the 4X 1.5 x45° chamfer to Bore R only.

clarifications.question: Confirm the actual through-depth/axial length for the Ø31.10/30.90 bores and Ø9.00 holes. The extraction interprets 15.0 mm as the large-bore axial width and 17.50 mm as the Ø9-hole lug thickness.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Depth is required for hole and bore geometry; the raster makes the association of these nearby dimensions moderately ambiguous.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use 15.0 mm axial length for large socket bores and 17.50 mm through thickness for the Ø9.00 holes.

clarifications.question: Clarify the meaning and final applicability of the '2X Ø28.94 I/P SOCKET CLEARANCE' callout and the adjacent 'Ø10.25/9.85' feature near the right Ø9 hole.
clarifications.why_it_matters: These may be in-process or clearance features rather than final machined geometry; their status affects the feature graph and tolerance requirements.
clarifications.suggested_default: Keep Ø28.94 as an in-process socket-clearance feature and Ø10.25/9.85 as a low-confidence coaxial counterbore/clearance until confirmed.

clarifications.question: Please verify feature F010 (Ø10.25/9.85 clearance/counterbore on right Ø9 hole). It was extracted with low confidence (0.55). Confirm whether this feature exists and its dimensions are correct.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Low-confidence features may be misread dimensions or hallucinated geometry. Agent 2 needs accurate data to plan machining operations.

guardrail_results.message: Title block was read first, but material and revision/issue fields are partially legible only.
No ordinate dimensioning was identified. Envelope was taken from clear overall dimensions 234.88, 149.94, and 55.00 rather than unrelated callouts.
Self-check found no M2/M2.5/M3/M4/M5/M6/M8/M10/M12 or other thread callouts on the visible drawing.
Hole/bore features were created only from callouts with a visible Ø symbol or a section-labeled bore. Repeated non-diameter values such as 3X (6.0) were not treated as holes.
Depths for the main bores and Ø9 holes were inferred from nearby section/plan dimensions where explicit THRU/depth symbols were not clear. Ø28.94 I/P socket-clearance depth remains unknown.
Section cuts A-A/B-B were separated from datum symbols. Datum A is clear; datum B is moderately clear; datum C is inferred from GD&T references and should be confirmed.
Every feature node has at least one relationship, symmetric relationships were added for opposite_face/parallel/coaxial pairs, and GD&T datum references point to datum features in the datum scheme.

analysis_trace.summary: The drawing is a single engineering sheet with main side/profile view at lower centre, plan/top view at middle, SECTION A-A at upper centre/left, SECTION B-B at right, an isometric reference view at upper right, notes at lower left, and title block at lower right.
issues: Some title block text and fine GD&T symbols are partially legible at the provided raster scale.

analysis_trace.summary: Identified the bottom main side/profile view as X-Z, the middle long view as a plan/top view, SECTION A-A as a section through the socket/body region, SECTION B-B as a section through Bore R, and the isometric view as reference only.
issues: The 3D relationship between the Section A-A bores and Bore R is clear enough to model but the exact count/scope of similar bore chamfers should be confirmed.

analysis_trace.summary: Extracted the overall cast body, datum A mounting face, upper face, two Ø35 boss collars, two Ø31.10/30.90 bores, Bore R Ø31.10/30.90, Ø28.94 I/P socket clearance, two Ø9.00 locating/datum holes, an interpreted Ø10.25/9.85 counterbore/clearance, central web, small 3X (6.0) pads, drafted faces, R25/R10 radii, Bore R chamfers, general edge break, lower pads/steps, and Ø9-hole lugs.
issues: The Ø10.25/9.85 feature and Ø28.94 I/P socket clearance have uncertain final-functional interpretation.

analysis_trace.summary: Correlated the plan/top Ø35 boss collars and central web with the sectioned Ø31 bores; correlated Bore R from SECTION B-B with the main/isometric view; correlated datum A from the side view with GD&T references in sections.
issues: Exact bore axial depth is inferred from nearby 15.0 width because no explicit THRU/depth text is visible.

analysis_trace.summary: Built a connected feature graph using the casting body as the main parent, coaxial links between boss collars and bores/clearances, intersecting links for through bores and pads, perpendicular links for Ø9 holes through lugs, and machining_dependency links for radii/chamfers/edge breaks.
issues: Some relationships involving internal cast faces are inferred from section geometry rather than explicit labels.

analysis_trace.summary: Embedded dimensional limits and GD&T into feature nodes: Ø31.10/30.90 limits on large bores, Ø9.00 hole dimensions and GD&T, Bore R position Ø0.5(M) to A/B/C, datum A flatness 0.25, chamfer tolerance 1.5 +0.10/-0.25 x45°, and general profile 1.5 to A/B/C.
issues: Several fine GD&T frame characters are partially legible; low-confidence GD&T entries were flagged in clarifications.

analysis_trace.summary: Separated section identifiers A-A/B-B from datum symbols. Datum A is the flat bottom mounting face. Datum B is interpreted as the right Ø9.00 hole axis. Datum C is interpreted as the paired left Ø9.00 hole axis due to C(M) datum references.
issues: Datum C symbol is not clearly visible and is inferred.

analysis_trace.summary: Captured material raw text from title block, general casting/defect notes, flash trimming/grinding note, draft note, unspecified R1.0 note, general profile 1.5 note, minimum wall thickness 2.5 note, and edge break 0.5 max note.
issues: General tolerance table/title-block standard text is only partly legible; no heat treatment/coating/packaging requirements were found.

analysis_trace.summary: Performed thread completeness, hole/feature completeness, depth, dimension re-read, relationship completeness, datum reference, and envelope sanity checks. No threads were found. All Ø-symbol callouts visible were represented or deliberately interpreted as non-hole geometry if not a diameter callout. The envelope uses overall dimensions, not repeated reference/counter dimensions.
issues: Depths for several holes/bores and exact datum C/GD&T text remain clarification items.
